25.reason: Missing spaces between numbers; should be printf("%d ", b[i]) to match required output format

30.reason: Variable j is not reinitialized to 0 each recursive call; should be "for(j=0;j<n;j++)" to iterate from start

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Variable j is not reinitialized to 0 each recursive call; should be "for(j=0;j<n;j++)" to iterate from start
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Variable j is not reinitialized to 0 for each recursive call; should be "for(j=0;j<n;j++)" to correctly iterate through all indices
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop variable j is initialized to 0 outside the loop but re-declared implicitly in C89/C90 may cause undefined behavior or compiler warning; more critically, this loop does not reset j properly if used multiple times, though here it's only called once. However, the real issue is that the loop condition uses j<n but j starts at 0 only because of prior initialization. This is fragile but not the main bug. The primary bug is the missing space in output.]
